title: ClientDistributedCacheManager and DistributedCache Interaction
description:
The 'filecache' directory in Hadoop MapReduce architecture primarily houses the 'ClientDistributedCacheManager.java' and 'DistributedCache.java' files. The former, 'ClientDistributedCacheManager.java', is responsible for managing the internal configuration of the cache for job submission. It determines timestamps of files to be cached and stores them in the job configuration, while also checking the visibility of distributed cache files and archives. 

On the other hand, 'DistributedCache.java' facilitates the distribution of large, read-only files needed by MapReduce applications across a Hadoop cluster. It adds files and archives to be cached, retrieves them, and manages their timestamps, ensuring they are copied only once per job and are accessible by all nodes in the cluster. 

These two components collaborate by using 'ClientDistributedCacheManager' to manage cache configurations and 'DistributedCache' to distribute these cached files across the cluster. The former provides the necessary configuration details, while the latter ensures these files are efficiently disseminated and accessible to all tasks in the MapReduce job. This collaboration exemplifies a well-defined architectural pattern of separation of concerns, where one component handles configuration management and the other focuses on file distribution, thereby enhancing the overall efficiency and scalability of Hadoop MapReduce jobs.
